response:
ISA, assume Walmart.com was developing a cross-border trade e-commerce marketplace integration platform. The platform aimed to streamline the process of online selling by small merchants in one country and online buying of those products across international borders by international shoppers, integrating Walmart Ecommerce USA’s marketplace (walmart.com) with multiple of its Walmart International Online Marketplaces (e.g., Walmart U.K. (walmart.uk), Walmart Canada (walmart.ca), Walmart Mexico (walmart.mx)) and also with the international marketplaces of Walmart’s Strategic Partners like JD.com (China) and Rakuten.com (Japan). Top-tier U.K. companies already on the platform include STATSports, which has grown from its home base in Northern Ireland to be a global leader in wearable tech for professional and amateur sportspeople; Glasgow-based BuyBox, which sells home and garden products; Nodor, which manufactures and sells Red Dragon darts and other sporting equipment from its headquarters in Bridgend, Wales; and Leicester-based Pertemba, which exports a wide array of products to 18 countries worldwide.  

**Cross-Border Trade CBT E-commerce Marketplace Integration Platform Overview**:  
- *Purpose*: To develop a unified e-commerce platform that enables efficient B2B2C cross-border trade.  
- *Target Users*: Sellers and buyers in various international markets, including small businesses and large enterprises.  
- *Key Goals*: Enhance user experience, improve operational efficiency, and ensure compliance with international trade laws.  
The cross-border trade CBT e-commerce marketplace platform also includes Cross-border Fulfillment Services. Walmart Fulfillment Services (WFS) offers end-to-end fulfillment services for Walmart Marketplace sellers. With one of the world’s largest supply chains, Walmart, and now building on it, Walmart’s Cross-Border Trade E-Commerce Marketplace Integration Platform is uniquely positioned to provide the scale, quality, cost, and efficiency cross-border online marketplace sellers need to grow a successful business on any of Walmart’s global marketplaces.  

For example, Canada-based entrepreneurs and e-commerce sellers can take advantage of simplified onboarding, streamlined fulfillment, targeted promotions, integrated financial services, and other support to help them rapidly scale and grow in the U.S. Since Canada-based sellers must be able to provide fast and reliable fulfillment delivered with duties prepaid (DDP), the WFS would be a substantial value-add to those sellers.  

**Fulfillment Model 1: Ship with Walmart Cross-Border Trade E-Commerce Marketplace (Export Hub) Model**:  
Walmart Cross-Border
